name: Tokushima Prefecture (Japan)

colors.symbolism: indigo — Tokushima is famous for its indigo (ai) dyeing

description: An indigo-blue field charged at the centre with a yellow emblem — a stylised character 'to' of Tokushima, shaped like a flying bird.
history: Tokushima's prefectural symbol was adopted on 18 March 1966. The indigo field refers to the region's long history of indigo (ai) dyeing; the emblem is a stylised form of the character 'to' from 徳島 (Tokushima), drawn as a flying bird to suggest harmony and progress.

features.symbolism: indigo, for the prefecture's indigo-dyeing heritage

features.role: Tokushima prefectural emblem (a stylised 'to' shaped like a flying bird)
